observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Treasurer continue to make representation and follow up with the depository bank, and request copies of the credit and debit memoranda and provide a copy to the Accountant for proper adjustment, if warranted; b. Municipal Accountant analyze the unadjusted items to the books, as warranted; and c. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile their cash records and settle the disparity in the balances of cash accounts per GL, cashbooks and BRS.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure proper justification and reconciliation of the balances with data from the Actual Balances of Stall Owners and Certified List of Stallholders occupying the Mansalay Public Market, and adopt measures to ensure accuracy in reporting of delinquencies from market operations.

We recommended that the: a. Municipal Accountant provide in 2025 justification regarding the different methods used in recording inventories during the year; and b. OIC-GSO and Municipal Accountant (i) maintain supplies stock cards and ledger cards for each inventory account; (ii) conduct physical count of inventories and prepare the RPCI per account; and (iii) maintain proper property records.

We recommended that the: a. Inventory Committee (i) conduct a full physical count and technical valuation of the property, plant and equipment to establish the existence, ownership and condition of the properties owned by the Municipality, and (ii) prepare the RPCPPE; b. Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate coordinate and prepare proper supporting records and ensure complete documentation for PPE and road projects; c. GSO-Designate prepare and maintain complete Property Cards, Local Road Property Cards and reconcile with Registry of Public Infrastructures; and d. Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and GSO with complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) in coordination with other concerned offices, analyze and conduct a thorough review of the Accounts Payable and Other Payables outstanding for more than two years to determine the validity of the claims, and effect the necessary adjustments pursuant to Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

We recommended that the: a. Municipal Accountant (i) make final demand to the recipient, other NGAs, and LGUs with outstanding unliquidated financial assistance to submit liquidation reports or enforce any existing agreement between the Municipality and the grantee; (ii) reconcile with the Municipal Agriculturist’s record regarding negative balances of Other Receivables and make appropriate adjustments, if warranted; and (iii) analyze and review the receivable accounts under Due from Other Funds and collate supporting documents to facilitate the settlement; b. Concerned offices to provide justification for the non-execution of the Memorandum of Agreement with the recipient LGUs regarding the financial assistance granted; and c. Municipal Accountant, Treasurer, MSWDO and Agriculturist exert continuous effort in retrieving relevant documents to support the long outstanding receivables recorded under Other Receivables account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert continuous effort to collate and analyze the details about the long outstanding Due to NGAs and LGUs accounts to determine the necessary actions to be taken regarding these unutilized government funds, and reconcile the Due to/from Other Funds accounts and effect the necessary adjustments.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require concerned municipal employees to provide justification for the noted delays in the liquidation of cash advances and frequent use of non-customary modes of transportation, and submit the same to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the: a. BAC Chairman, thru the BAC Technical Working Group and Secretariat, explain the noted lapses specifically on the delays in the procurement process, inconsistencies in the dates indicated in various documents, and absence of necessary procurement documents; b. BAC strictly observe the period of action provided in RA 9184 and its Revised IRR within which certain stages of the procurement process must be completed; c. Municipal Engineer explain the noted observations on project start date inconsistent with NTP effectivity and submit evidence of compliance with Section 37.4.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in monitoring contract implementation and computation of delay, if any; d. Municipal Accountant impose liquidated damages to contractors and suppliers with noted delays in project implementation/delivery pursuant to Section 68 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and submit proof of its settlement; and e. Municipal Accountant, BAC, and the Municipal Engineer explain the deficiencies in supporting documents and submit the lacking documents.

We recommended that the: a. Municipal Development Council, Implementing Office and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (i) plan the timely procurement and implementation of the identified priority development projects under the 20% DF and institute necessary actions for its effective implementation; (ii) coordinate with concerned Implementing Offices and Barangays; (iii) ensure barangay counterpart funds are available; and (iv) confirm feasibility of PPAs; b. Local Development Office and Municipal Planning Coordinator coordinate and close 2025 and monitor progress; c. Municipal Engineer follow strictly the implementation period as indicated in the AIP/PPMP; and d. Municipal Engineer and the BAC provide periodically the MBO and the MAccO with the status of projects and procurements undertaken by the Municipality.

We recommended that the: a. MDRRM Officer and concerned municipal officials conduct a thorough review of the unimplemented projects to identify the cause of delays and address the issues to implement the projects so that the benefits from the project could be realized; b. MPDC, MDRRMC and MDRRM Officer provide SMART performance indicators; c. Municipal Budget Officer, MPDC, and Committee on Appropriation-Sangguniang Bayan review and revisit the budgetary documents and submit copies to the Audit Team; d. MAccO and the staff in-charge of the preparation of LDRRMF Utilization Report analyze the difference in their report and effect the necessary adjustments; and e. MAccO provide justification on the noted observations regarding the non-disclosures in the compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2014-002.

2.9  We recommended and the Management, through the Municipal Accounting Office, agreed to prepare the necessary journal entries to transfer the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the special trust fund account. Henceforth, ensure that transfers of unexpended balance of LDRRM funds to the Trust Fund are recorded correctly in the books of accounts for fair presentation in the financial statements.
*Cash in Bank - Local Currency, Current Account if actual cash transfer is made
**Prior Period Adjustment if the unexpended balance pertains to prior year LDRRM funds

3.9  We recommended and the Management agreed to immediately investigate the noted discrepancies starting from the latest financial statements and prepare necessary adjusting entries to immediately correct the errors incurred in recording. Also, for the succeeding periods, conduct regular reconciliation of reciprocal accounts and immediately investigate and correct noted deficiencies to avoid accumulation of unaccounted amounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed that through the Municipal Accounting Office to immediately investigate the noted discrepancies in the recorded Loans Payable-Domestic account and effect the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balance of the loans payable.
Moving forward, we recommended and the Management further committed  to regularly refer to the Statement of Account submitted by loan creditors as to monitoring of the balances and immediately investigate and correct records, if warranted.

6.9  We recommended and the Management agreed to prioritize the reconciliation of the remittance files with the billing files to address the outstanding discrepancies. Re-evaluate the tasks of LGU personnel particularly the job order workers and assign personnel who will concentrate on document retrieval. Upon the return to duty of the HRMO Officer, prioritize the reconciliation and updating of service records. Closely coordinate with the GSIS and submit the necessary service records and remittance files to substantiate the remittances made.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Specify the duties and responsibilities of the job order workers in their contracts of service and ensure that accomplishment reports complement their specified tasks; also, define substantially the term teaching overload and cite instances when the regular teaching load becomes an overload in the contract of service for instructors;
Thoroughly evaluate the granting of travel orders of the job order workers only to those that are necessary and those that cannot be performed by a permanent employee of the agency; and
Discontinue paying reimbursement of expenses to job order workers and course the reimbursements and disbursements through proper disbursing officers.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Require the LDRRMO to prepare the annual LDRRMF Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) in conformity with the prescribed format. Review and update the LDRRMFIP to properly reflect the projects and activities based on status of the fund.
Further, require the Municipal Accountant to properly disclose the LDRRM Funds in the Notes to Financial Statement in conformity with the prescribed format in Annexes A and D of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

14.9  We recommended and the Management agreed to discontinue the practice of granting cash advances to elected officials other than for the sole purpose of travel.  Special purpose cash advances should be properly coursed through the Municipal Treasurer or Special Disbursing Officer.

We recommended that Management:

Conduct immediate investigation on the nature of these reconciling items totaling ₱1.401 million and effect adjusting entries, if necessary.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Improve the procurement planning of the LGU and to properly implement the procurement procedures provided in RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations; and

Discourage the practice of procuring items beforehand then processing it for reimbursement, limit the processing of reimbursement for inevitable, emergency and immaterial transactions only.

We recommended that the Municipality immediately discontinue the practice of splitting purchase orders to circumvent the law on procurement. Strictly adhere to the laws, rules and regulations in the procurement of goods and services such that competitive public bidding be adopted in all its procurement activities and resort to alternative methods of procurement only if the condition set forth under Section 48 to 53 of R.A. No. 9184 are met, to promote economy and efficiency in operations.  Should the LGU decide to adopt an alternative mode of procurement, ensure that necessary posting requirements are met.

Conduct immediate investigation on the nature of these reconciling items totaling ₱1.401 million and effect adjusting entries, if necessary.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to:

Through the accounting office, to appropriately record the Leave Benefits Payable and Terminal Leave Benefits of the Agency;
Include the leave benefits payable in budgeting considerations for the agency to have sufficient cash funding for the leave benefits of its employees. Should the management find it difficult to immediately fund the whole amount of payable, it is recommended to prioritize the current year amounts; and
Maximize the revenue-raising power of the municipality to identify the additional source of revenue to fund the leave benefits payable.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to:

Through the accounting office, to appropriately record the Leave Benefits Payable and Terminal Leave Benefits of the Agency;
Include the leave benefits payable in budgeting considerations for the agency to have sufficient cash funding for the leave benefits of its employees. We recommended to the Management to:
Through the accounting office, to appropriately record the Leave Benefits Payable and Terminal Leave Benefits of the Agency;
Include the leave benefits payable in budgeting considerations for the agency to have sufficient cash funding for the leave benefits of its employees. Should the management find it difficult to immediately fund the whole amount of payable, it is recommended to prioritize the current year amounts; and
Maximize the revenue-raising power of the municipality to identify the additional source of revenue to fund the leave benefits payable.

We recommended to the Management to provide the identified lacking documents. In the succeeding similar transactions of the LGU, ensure that pertinent documentary requirements are provided prior to any disbursement.

We recommended to the Management to:
provide justifications why these transactions should be considered expenses chargeable against the Special Education Fund;
submit a letter or any equivalent document from the Department of Education – Salay, showing the budgetary requirements of their activities and proof that the amount for the prizes and honorarium of the judges provided by the LGU is already net of the budgetary provision of the DepEd; and
submit photos of the activities conducted and the tally sheets used by the judges in determining the winner for each activity.
In the succeeding liquidations of the LGU for similar activities, we also recommended submission of proof/photos of the activities conducted, tally sheets used by the judges and any available document supporting the conduct of the activity.
We further recommended to the Management to disburse amount from the Special Education Fund only for those allowable expenses as enumerated in Deped-DBM-DILG Joint Memoranda Circular Nos. 01 s.2017, 01 s.2020, and 02 s.2020.

We recommended to the Management to provide the team with sufficient legal basis authorizing the payment of the rewards and incentives the these PNP Personnel. Further, we recommend to the Management to discontinue granting similar payments unless supported/authorized by law.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Mayor through the Accounting Unit to:

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor through the BAC and TWG to:

a.	Carefully examine eligibility documents; by cautiously evaluating the legal, technical and financial documents submitted by bidders during preliminary evaluation and post-qualification before recommending the award of the contract; 

b.	Strengthen post qualification procedures and activities in compliance with Rule X of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. This may include but not limited to the following, the Procurement Unit maintaining an updated database/ schedule of contractors and their track records (completed works) with the LGU which shall be readily available during conduct of every post qualification for purposes of verifying outstanding works; and

c.	Impose administrative sanctions/penalties to the contractors/suppliers for the submission of eligibility requirements containing false information or falsified documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
Intensify tax collection campaign to maximize collection of RPT due to the municipal government in order to increase the delivery of basic services and facilities; and
Send demand letters to the delinquent taxpayers and avail of the remedies through levy on real property or judicial actions provided under the Local Government Code of 1991
observations.reason: On going collection

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Management to:
Through the accounting office, to appropriately record the Leave Benefits Payable and Terminal Leave Benefits of the Agency;
Include the leave benefits payable in budgeting considerations for the agency to have sufficient cash funding for the leave benefits of its employees. Should the management find it difficult to immediately fund the whole amount of payable, it is recommended to prioritize the current year amounts; and
Maximize the revenue-raising power of the municipality to identify additional source of revenue to fund the leave benefits payable.

We recommended to the Mayor through the Accounting Unit to:
Set up allowance for impairment of ₱113,398.00 for Dormant Accounts and submit Journal Voucher as supporting document; and
Comply with Section 8.2 and 8.3 and submit schedule of Dormant Receivable for writing-off with the required supporting documents such as but not limited to submission of available Memorandum of Agreements (MOA), communication letter to Source Agency for Dormant accounts for writing- off and proof of efforts exerted for collection.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor through the BAC and TWG to:
Carefully examine eligibility documents by cautiously evaluating the legal, technical and financial documents submitted by bidders during preliminary evaluation and post-qualification before recommending the award of the contract;
Strengthen post qualification procedures and activities in compliance with Rule X of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. This may include but not limited to the following, the Procurement Unit maintaining an updated database/ schedule of contractors and their track records (completed works) with the LGU which shall be readily available during conduct of every post qualification for purposes of verifying outstanding works; and
Impose administrative sanctions/penalties to the contractors/suppliers for the submission of eligibility requirements containing false information or falsified documents.

We recommended that Management should furnish the Audit Team the copy of contract and its supporting documents within five days after its perfection pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001. Otherwise, we recommended that appropriate sanctions as provided for in Section 4.0 of the same circular be imposed on responsible officials/personnel.

We recommended that for evaluation and monitoring purposes, management direct officials concerned to prepare and submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment (LDRRMFP) as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 indicating the 30% allocation of QRF and the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness showing the   projects and activities to be funded.
We recommended that Management submit an explanation to the difference of the amount transferred to the Trust Fund Bank account and to the unutilized/unexpended balance of CY 2017 LDRRMF.

We recommended that Management should direct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salary of the concerned Accountable Officers with unliquidated balances without prejudice to the filing of appropriate sanctions as provided for under the above-mentioned provisions 

Also, it is recommended that the Management should discontinue the practice of granting cash advance/s to any official or employees without first settling or liquidation thereof has been made of the previous cash advance.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
Intensify tax collection campaign to maximize collection of RPT due to the municipal government in order to increase the delivery of basic services and facilities; and
Send demand letters to the delinquent taxpayers and avail of the remedies through levy on real property or judicial actions provided under the Local Government Code of 1991

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management should furnish the Audit Team the copy of contract and its supporting documents within five days after its perfection pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001. Otherwise, we recommended that appropriate sanctions as provided for in Section 4.0 of the same circular be imposed on responsible officials/personnel.

Recommendations:
We recommended that in order to reduce the delay in the release of barangay RPT Shares the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer:
Closely monitor and expedite the preparation and processing of Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) and Payrolls and other documents pertaining to the release of RPT shares;
Coordinate in identifying other bottleneck in the process flow that cause/s the delay in the release of RPT shares and take remedial measures to mitigate, if not eliminate, the same.

Recommendation:
We recommended that for evaluation and monitoring purposes, management direct officials concerned to prepare and submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment (LDRRMFP) as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 indicating the 30% allocation of QRF and the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness showing the   projects and activities to be funded.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management submit an explanation to the difference of the amount transferred to the Trust Fund Bank account and to the unutilized/unexpended balance of CY 2017 LDRRMF.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management should direct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salary of the concerned Accountable Officers with unliquidated balances without prejudice to the filing of appropriate sanctions as provided for under the above-mentioned provisions Also, it is recommended that the Management should discontinue the practice of granting cash advance/s to any official or employees without first settling or liquidation thereof has been made of the previous cash advance.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management:
Ensure completeness and propriety of the required supporting documents for all contracts submitted to COA for review in accordance with COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009;
Require the personnel in-charge in the submission of the contracts and its supporting documents to maintain a checklist of required documents for each of the transactions to serve as a guide to ensure the completeness of the documents submitted to COA for review;
Employ means to ensure timely submission to COA of any POs or contracts entered into by the Municipality to suppliers.

Management Comments:
During the Exit Conference, the management committed to comply with this audit observation. The management also assures COA that complete set will be given.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management should furnish the Audit Team the copy of contract and its supporting documents within five days after its perfection pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001. Otherwise, we recommended that appropriate sanctions as provided for in Section 4.0 of the same circular be imposed on responsible officials/personnel.

Management Comments:
During the Exit Conference, the management committed to comply with this audit observation.

Recommendations:
We recommended that in order to reduce the delay in the release of barangay RPT Shares the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer:
Closely monitor and expedite the preparation and processing of Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) and Payrolls and other documents pertaining to the release of RPT shares;
Coordinate in identifying other bottleneck in the process flow that cause/s the delay in the release of RPT shares and take remedial measures to mitigate, if not eliminate, the same.

Management Comments:
RPT shares of Province, Municipal and Barangay are computed manually which affects the releases of RPT shares which contributed the delay on preparation and release of RPT shares of barangay for the periods of 4th quarter CY 2018 to 2nd quarter of CY 2019.
As to the cited Section 271 (d) of R. A. 7160, we earnestly request  to be given consideration for any slight delay of the release of the RPT shares to the barangay at this time for we are still using the manual computation system. We however assure your end that we are committed to improve our process flow so we could facilitate the release of the RPT shares of the barangays within a reasonable period of time.

Recommendation:
We recommended that for evaluation and monitoring purposes, management direct officials concerned to prepare and submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment (LDRRMFP) as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 indicating the 30% allocation of QRF and the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness showing the   projects and activities to be funded.

Management Comments:
The management agrees to instruct the newly appointed Local Disaster Risk Officer to comply with what is required in the COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 in order to evaluate and monitor allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management submit an explanation to the difference of the amount transferred to the Trust Fund Bank account and to the unutilized/unexpended balance of CY 2017 LDRRMF.

Management Comments:
On CY 2017, the LGU transfer only the actual amount derived from revenue collection of the Municipality. There was no clear interpretation of the memorandum circular during that time of whether the actual or budgeted should be used. Rest assured that for 2018 and onwards, unexpended balance of the LDRRMF was transferred even if collection was not 100%.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
require the Municipal Treasurer to adhere strictly to the provision that all payment should be made by check directly payable to suppliers except in cases where needed items are extremely necessary and urgent which cannot be conveniently paid by check;
stop granting reimbursement to all Municipal Officials and Employees, unless the item/service is petty in nature;
ensure that the proper procurement purchases are implemented by the Department Head responsible for procurement.

Management Comments:
Management committed to comply with this audit observation and to stop the granting of reimbursement to all Municipal Officials and employees. The management also ensures that proper procurement purchases are implemented by the Department Head responsible for procurement.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management should direct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salary of the concerned Accountable Officers with unliquidated balances without prejudice to the filing of appropriate sanctions as provided for under the above-mentioned provisions Also, it is recommended that the Management should discontinue the practice of granting cash advance/s to any official or employees without first settling or liquidation thereof has been made of the previous cash advance.

Management Comments:
During the Exit Conference, the management committed to comply with this audit observation. Also, the Municipal Accountant also ensures the strict implementation of the submission of liquidation for all cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the officials concerned to stop the practice of splitting of contracts and strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 in all the procurements. We further require the Management to submit justification on the above-mentioned transactions for evaluation.

We recommended that Management:

Submit justification on the non-conduct of competitive public bidding.

Observe the thresholds in the procurement of goods and implementation of infrastructure projects. Resort to public bidding when the procurement exceeded the threshold amount. 

Require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to adhere to the provisions of R.A. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations.

We recommended that management:

Submit justification on the noted deficiencies.
Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that complete supporting documents are attached to the Disbursement Vouchers before processing and approving payments or issuing checks to guarantee that only valid claims will be paid pursuant to existing provisions of law.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that complete supporting documents are attached to the Disbursement Vouchers before processing and approving payments or issuing checks to guarantee that only valid claims will be paid pursuant to existing provisions of law. Also, liquidation reports for cash advances should always be substantiated with Official Receipts or proof of payments.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly exhaust all possible remedies for purposes of causing the immediate liquidation of transferred funds to concern government agencies. Order the return of unused monies. This could be initiated by the sending of letters to defaulting agencies requesting for submission of necessary documents proving the utilization or non-use of transferred funds. In addition, to establish accurate amount of receivables, the accounting of said accounts shall be thoroughly checked as to validity which shall start with the investigation of abnormal balances.
As for the funds received by the Municipality from other government agencies which remained outstanding for more than 3 years, exhaustive effort should be made to locate the records and submit the needed documents to fully liquidate the money. If papers can no longer be recovered, request for write-off of accounts shall be sent to the COA Commission Proper so that adjustments in recording on the agency’s book of accounts may be effected.

We recommended that the Management immediately increase the Fidelity Bond of the designated AO of the Municipality to amount corresponding to his maximum cash handled in accordance with the revised schedule of premium rates of fidelity bond as presented in Annex C issued by the Bureau of Treasury and strictly adhere to the provisions of the Bureau of Treasury Circular 02-2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
Intensify tax collection campaign to maximize collection of RPT due to the municipal government in order to increase the delivery of basic services and facilities; and
Send demand letters to the delinquent taxpayers and avail of the remedies through levy on real property or judicial actions provided under the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.reason: Still on-going collection

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) be guided by the provisions of Sec. 272 of RA 7160 and the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular in the preparation of the SEF Annual Budget and identify the priority expenditures pursuant thereto. Any issues or conflicts arising from its implementation and cases not covered by the provisions shall be submitted to the DepEd for resolution and in consultation with the municipal government.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of charging to LDRRM QRF expenses which are not supported by Local Sangguniang Resolution and declaration of state of calamity to adhere strictly to the guidelines set under Section 2 of RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the officials concerned to stop the practice of splitting of contracts and strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 in all the procurements. We further require the Management to submit justification on the above-mentioned transactions for evaluation.

We recommended that Management:

Submit justification on the non-conduct of competitive public bidding.

Observe the thresholds in the procurement of goods and implementation of infrastructure projects. Resort to public bidding when the procurement exceeded the threshold amount. 

Require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to adhere to the provisions of R.A. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations.

We recommended that management:

Submit justification on the noted deficiencies.
Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that complete supporting documents are attached to the Disbursement Vouchers before processing and approving payments or issuing checks to guarantee that only valid claims will be paid pursuant to existing provisions of law.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that complete supporting documents are attached to the Disbursement Vouchers before processing and approving payments or issuing checks to guarantee that only valid claims will be paid pursuant to existing provisions of law. Also, liquidation reports for cash advances should always be substantiated with Official Receipts or proof of payments.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly exhaust all possible remedies for purposes of causing the immediate liquidation of transferred funds to concern government agencies. Order the return of unused monies. This could be initiated by the sending of letters to defaulting agencies requesting for submission of necessary documents proving the utilization or non-use of transferred funds. In addition, to establish accurate amount of receivables, the accounting of said accounts shall be thoroughly checked as to validity which shall start with the investigation of abnormal balances.
As for the funds received by the Municipality from other government agencies which remained outstanding for more than 3 years, exhaustive effort should be made to locate the records and submit the needed documents to fully liquidate the money. If papers can no longer be recovered, request for write-off of accounts shall be sent to the COA Commission Proper so that adjustments in recording on the agency’s book of accounts may be effected.

We recommended that the Management immediately increase the Fidelity Bond of the designated AO of the Municipality to amount corresponding to his maximum cash handled in accordance with the revised schedule of premium rates of fidelity bond as presented in Annex C issued by the Bureau of Treasury and strictly adhere to the provisions of the Bureau of Treasury Circular 02-2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
Intensify tax collection campaign to maximize collection of RPT due to the municipal government in order to increase the delivery of basic services and facilities; and
Send demand letters to the delinquent taxpayers and avail of the remedies through levy on real property or judicial actions provided under the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.reason: Still on-going collection

We recommended that Management submit immediately the required documents herein enumerated for review and to submit a written justification as to why it was not submitted within 5 working days from the execution of the contract by the agency.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the correct amount of Liquidated Damage to be deducted from the cost of the contract as mandated by Section 68 of Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant to 	withhold the correct amount of Liquidated Damage to be deducted from the cost 	of the contract as mandated by Section 68 of Revised IRR of RA 9184.
We also recommendedsupplying and filling out the essential documents completely and submitting those within the reglementary period.

We recommended that Management submit immediately the required documents herein enumerated for review and to submit a written justification as to why it was not submitted within 5 working days from the execution of the contract by the agency.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality should be responsive of the provisions of 	the legal bases through upholding compliance in the agreement disclosed in all 	contracts they entered into.
We also recommended that the LGU should submit justification elaborating reasons which hindered the LGU in accomplishing implemented projects on time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enforce strictly the refund of the unused/unspent and excess claim of cash advance to be acknowledged by the Municipal Treasurer thru the issuance of an Official Receipt which is to beattached to the Liquidation Report.

Also avoid the practice of deducting the excess claims and unspent cash advances from the succeeding cash advances and from the payroll as it creates difficulty in monitoring and audit.

Further, we recommended that additional cash advance shall only be granted once the previous one is fully liquidated.
We recommended that Management strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 on the requirements in the liquidation of cash advances for travel.

We recommended that Management should instruct the General Services Officer to furnish copies of Acceptance and Inspection Reports, together with the Delivery Receipts to the Audit Team within twenty-four hours from acceptance to enable them to conduct timely validation thereof pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002 dated May 18, 2009.

We recommended therefore that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Designated DRRM Officer to prioritize the preparation and submission of the LDRRMFIP, and the maintenance of public awareness through bulletin board.  The submission, and compliance of, these reports has basic importance on the lives of the constituents as it promotes disaster mitigation, prevention, and preparedness. 

In addition, the Designated DRRM Officer must consider the prompt fulfillment of the activities or tasks enumerated in the LDRRMFIP as the most effective way of handling disaster goes with the idea that outweighs preventing over curing.

We recommended that the Municipality evaluate further the components included in its 20% annual internal revenue allotment (IRA) for development projects to avoid incurring expenditures that are not related to and/or not connected with the implementation of development projects, programs and activities of the Municipality in conformity with the Joint DILG-DBM Memorandum Circular.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor make the appropriate action with respect to settlement of audit disallowances.

We recommended that the Municipal  Treasurer faithfully monitor and ensure compliance with Section 69 of P.D. 1445, COA and MOF Joint Circular 1-81 and Section 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs. We also recommend that administrative sanctions be imposed on all accountable officers who repeatedly did not perform their sworn duties in accordance with law and regulations.

We recommended that Management direct the heads of the different implementing offices to fully implement projects funded out of the 20% Local Development Fund during the year in order to promptly  provide the constituents the benefits due them.  Likewise, we recommended that the Municipality enforce strict compliance of Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2011-1 of the Department of Interior and Local Government Unit (DILG) and the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) dated April 13, 2011.

We recommended that Management exert extra efforts  to follow up the status of liquidation of cash advances from the Office of the Chairman, Commission on Audit  so that the amount of ₱1,500,000.00 representing the prior years’ cash advances for intelligence funds will be dropped from the books of account.
We further recommended for the immediate liquidation of cash advance on intelligence funds that were unliquidated as of December 31, 2014.

It is further recommended that cash advances for intelligence fund be liquidated within one (1) month from the date the purpose of the cash advance was accomplished, pursuant to  Circular No. 2003-003 dated July 30, 2003.  Subsequent cash advances for intelligence fund should be granted only after the issuance of credit advice from the Chairman of the Commission on Audit or the submission of liquidation vouchers/reports of the previous cash advance to the Chairman.

We recommended that Management direct the heads of the different implementing offices to fully implement projects funded out of the 20% Local Development Fund during the year  in order to promptly  provide the constituents the benefits due them.  

 Likewise, we recommended that the Municipality enforce strict compliance of Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2011-1 of the Department of Interior and Local Government Unit (DILG) and the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) dated April 13, 2011.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enforce strictly the refund of the unused/unspent and excess claim of cash advance to be acknowledged by the Municipal Treasurer thru the issuance of an Official Receipt which is to beattached to the Liquidation Report.
Also avoid the practice of deducting the excess claims and unspent cash advances from the succeeding cash advances and from the payroll as it creates difficulty in monitoring and audit.
Further, we recommended that additional cash advance shall only be granted once the previous one is fully liquidated.
We recommended that Management strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 on the requirements in the liquidation of cash advances for travel.

We recommended that Management should instruct the General Services Officer to furnish copies of Acceptance and Inspection Reports, together with the Delivery Receipts to the Audit Team within twenty-four hours from acceptance to enable them to conduct timely validation thereof pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002 dated May 18, 2009.

We recommended therefore that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Designated DRRM Officer to prioritize the preparation and submission of the LDRRMFIP, and the maintenance of public awareness through bulletin board.  The submission, and compliance of, these reports has basic importance on the lives of the constituents as it promotes disaster mitigation, prevention, and preparedness.
In addition, the Designated DRRM Officer must consider the prompt fulfillment of the activities or tasks enumerated in the LDRRMFIP as the most effective way of handling disaster goes with the idea that outweighs preventing over curing.

We recommended that the Municipality evaluate further the components included in its 20% annual internal revenue allotment (IRA) for development projects to avoid incurring expenditures that are not related to and/or not connected with the implementation of development projects, programs and activities of the Municipality in conformity with the Joint DILG-DBM Memorandum Circular.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor make the appropriate action with respect to settlement of audit disallowances.

We recommended  that management stop the practice of holding receptions or serving meals and snacks to guests and visitors and on during  meetings,  sports activities  and other events and the charging thereof to government funds other than to the representation allowance of officials concerned

We recommended that management should adhere to the regulation requiring checks drawn to be made payable directly to the creditor and to stop the practice of purchasing supplies on reimbursement basis to officials and employees and ensure the withholding of the required amount of taxes under existing regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal  Treasurer faithfully monitor and ensure compliance with Section 69 of P.D. 1445, COA and MOF Joint Circular 1-81 and Section 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs. We also recommend that administrative sanctions be imposed on all accountable officers who repeatedly did not perform their sworn duties in accordance with law and regulations.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer will implement strict observance to the rules and regulations governing remittances of collections arising from taxes, revenues another receipts in order not to jeopardize the finances of the Municipality of Salay.  There must be constant follow-up with regards to daily t collections and the same shall be deposited to the bank.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GAD Focal Person submit the targets and accomplishments for the year 2015 pursuant to Joint Circular No. 2004-1 dated April 5, 2004

We recommended that the provisions of the IRR of RA 9184 on the procurement of supplies and materials through shopping should be strictly observed.  Procurement should be based on the approved APP and procurement above the above the threshold should be through competitive bidding as required.

We recommended that Management direct the officials concerned to use the perpetual inventory method in recording supplies and materials, drugs and medicines and as provided in the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I for LGUs.

We recommended that Management direct the heads of the different implementing offices to fully implement projects funded out of the 20% Local Development Fund during the year in order to promptly  provide the constituents the benefits due them.  Likewise, we recommended that the Municipality enforce strict compliance of Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2011-1 of the Department of Interior and Local Government Unit (DILG) and the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) dated April 13, 2011.
observations.reason:
The low utilization of the 20% Development Fund was due to the following reasons:
1.The Construction of Health Center was realigned through Appropriation Ordinance Nos. 5 
The Municipality was able to access the DOH with funds for the same health center project.  The realignment was for the LGU counterpart to the 2015 BUB project.  The said projects were already implemented in 2015 but there was no disbursement yet as of the end of the year.  According to the Municipal Engineer, that as of this period there is already about 80% accomplishment of the road concreting .projects. Bidding for the BUB projects such as the OPDA and ICFP will be conducted on February 28, 2016.  
2.The Construction of the Multi-Purpose project is also on-going as of the end of the year.
3.The rehabilitation of the FMR projects are for the maintenance of our MRDP finished roads.
4.The Construction of Public Market was intended for Phase II of our Public Market but we were able to access the National Government through the DPWH.  This budget shall be utilized for whatever deficiencies later in the implementation of the project.
The Rehabilitation of Municipal road project was bidded twice already but there were no bidders.  Said procurement is already for negotiation because of failure of two biddings.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management exert extra efforts  to follow up the status of liquidation of cash advances from the Office of the Chairman, Commission on Audit  so that the amount of ₱1,500,000.00 representing the prior years’ cash advances for intelligence funds will be dropped from the books of account.
We further recommended for the immediate liquidation of cash advance on intelligence funds that were unliquidated as of December 31, 2014.

It is further recommended that cash advances for intelligence fund be liquidated within one (1) month from the date the purpose of the cash advance was accomplished, pursuant to  Circular No. 2003-003 dated July 30, 2003.  Subsequent cash advances for intelligence fund should be granted only after the issuance of credit advice from the Chairman of the Commission on Audit or the submission of liquidation vouchers/reports of the previous cash advance to the Chairman.
observations.reason: Follow-up with COA Central Office had been made regarding the submitted liquidation reports on Intelligence Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the heads of the different implementing offices to fully implement projects funded out of the 20% Local Development Fund during the year  in order to promptly  provide the constituents the benefits due them.  

 Likewise, we recommended that the Municipality enforce strict compliance of Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2011-1 of the Department of Interior and Local Government Unit (DILG) and the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) dated April 13, 2011.
observations.reason:
The  CY 2014 projects were not implemented due to the following reasons:
1.Part of the budget for the Construction of Multi-Purpose Building amounting to    P1,800,000.00 was realigned through Ordinance No. 10 dated September 5, 2014 for the Construction of Public Market.  Said Construction of Public market was already under construction as of this date.

The Management has likewise appropriated for 2015 additional budget for the Construction of Multi-Purpose Building which they hope to implement on 2015.

2.The construction of 2-barrel Box Culver with access road (PRDP) is for equity to the    above-mentioned project which is still pending with DPWH.

3.The Upgrading of FMR (BUB) Project is also for the equity of the above-mentioned      project which to this date is not yet downloaded to the LGU.

4.The BUB Livelihood projects are likewise project equity which are also not yet   downloaded to the LGU.

5.The Rehabilitation of Municipal Road Project is now in the procurement process

6. The Construction of the Municipal Road (Public Market Area) is not yet implemented since the Construction of the Public Market has just started.

7.The Loan Amortization for the Public Market was not implemented since the said loan was not yet released by the MDFO.
On the unimplemented projects for 2010 to 2013, the following were already in the procurement process, to wit:

Rehabilitation of Water System
Construction of Multi-Purpose Vault (Public Cemetery)
Construction of materials Recovery Facility

The other continuing appropriations were realigned, to wit:
Appropriation Ordinance No. 01 (₱81,000.00) for the Construction of Perimeter Fence;
Appropriation Ordinance No. 09 (₱78,500.00 for the Concreting of Coastal Road in Barangay Inobulan;
Appropriation Ordinance No. 10 (₱5,015,777.65) for the Construction of Public Market.

The Municipality of Salay had noted our recommendation to adhere strictly to the compliance of Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2011-1 of the Department of Interior and Local Government Unit (DILG) and the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) dated April 13, 2011.
